title: Getting started
text: 💡 Tip needs done choose project rather globally (user) every project need credentials set . Azure requires credentials set locally computer order data contains. number places information can stored use depends team’s use security level required. two .Renviron files, one can stored project folder one can set globally project can access credential. files need created set can either done manually , conveniently, using usethis package: ⚠️ Important Don’t forget ensure .gitignore .Renviron listed. particularly important file saved project. opening .Renviron file first time, whether user project, blank following needs added: = sign add URL Azure endpoint (quotes). changes files require restart R use usethis function remind message appears Console. 💡 Tip use .Renviron files project read user. Ensure project .Renviron completely blank though default blank even details user file.
code: # creates or opens an existing file in the project usethis::edit_r_environ(scope = "project") # Add a file to .gitignore if you haven't already done so usethis::use_git_ignore(".Renviron")  # creates or opens an existing file in the global usethis::edit_r_environ(scope = "user")  # just running the following defaults to "user" usethis::edit_r_environ() AZ_STORAGE_EP=

title: Getting started

Ensure project .Renviron completely blank though default blank even details user file.
code: # creates or opens an existing file in the project usethis::edit_r_environ(scope = "project") # Add a file to .gitignore if you haven't already done so usethis::use_git_ignore(".Renviron")  # creates or opens an existing file in the global usethis::edit_r_environ(scope = "user")  # just running the following defaults to "user" usethis::edit_r_environ() AZ_STORAGE_EP=

what: Find what containers there available to view
title: Getting started
text: see containers access :
code: library(azkit) # This will work if you have the AZ_STORAGE_EP url stored in an `.Renviron` file azkit::list_container_names()  # The default is the same as writing regardless of which `.Renviron` file is used azkit::list_container_names(Sys.getenv("AZ_STORAGE_EP"))  # If you are using keyring you will need to type out the code: azkit::list_container_names(endpoint_url = keyring::key_get("AZ_STORAGE_EP"))

title: Getting started
text: Two functions used together access files particular container. 💡 Tip following refer "supporting-data" placeholder need changed container access . function list_files() returns files according type, rather individual files may familiar list files: list_files() requires get_container() point exact container provide relevant credentials: container sub folders list_files() can also return files recursive = parameter set TRUE (default FALSE): 💡 Tip ’d prefer view Azure GUI (Graphical User Interface) permissions use Microsoft program Azure Storage Explorer explore containers access via browser logging https://portal.azure.com/#home
code: # library(here)  # This is just an example of a base R function that returns all the files # list.files(here::here()) # "supporting_data" is a name that you will have to change according to what the # containers you have access to and what they are called azkit::list_files(   get_container(     container_name = "supporting-data",     endpoint_url = Sys.getenv("AZ_STORAGE_EP")   ),   ext = "csv" ) azkit::list_files(   get_container(     container_name = "supporting-data",     endpoint_url = Sys.getenv("AZ_STORAGE_EP")   ),   ext = "csv",   recursive = TRUE )

title: Getting started
text: functions get particular files, need used conjunction get_container() function provides details container credentials. simplest access downloading csv: can also use arguments applied specific file reader argument .... example, readr::read_delim() used function read_azure_csv ’s possible use arguments readr::read_delim() use: Functions file types follow pattern using get_container() detailing file wanted: download formats familiar data required raw binary form stored Azure parsed/translated means following function retrieve data type: makes transferring data fast can translated locally. example .csv look like:
code: # Note this code is "nested" so the get_container() function is inside # the read_azure_csv() function  azkit::read_azure_csv(   azkit::get_container(     container_name = "supporting-data",     endpoint_url = Sys.getenv("AZ_STORAGE_EP")   ),   file = "mitigator-lookup.csv" ) # Note this code is "piped" so the get_container() function is before the # the read_azure_csv() function  azkit::get_container(   container_name = "supporting-data",   endpoint_url = Sys.getenv("AZ_STORAGE_EP") ) |>   azkit::read_azure_csv(     file = "mitigator-lookup.csv",     # select specific columns and can be used with other dplyr functions     col_select = dplyr::starts_with("active")   ) azkit::read_azure_rds() azkit::read_azure_json() azkit::read_azure_jsongz() azkit::read_azure_parquet() # This is not a human readable form!  azkit::read_azure_file(   azkit::get_container(     container_name = "supporting-data",     endpoint_url = Sys.getenv("AZ_STORAGE_EP")   ),   file = "mitigator-lookup.csv" ) raw_data <- azkit::read_azure_file(   azkit::get_container(     container_name = "supporting-data",     endpoint_url = Sys.getenv("AZ_STORAGE_EP")   ),   file = "mitigator-lookup.csv" )  csv_data <- read.csv(text = rawToChar(raw_data))  # view top few rows of what is now a data frame head(csv_data)

title: Getting started
text: functions don’t read multiple files can combined code using loops equivalent using purrr package:
code: list_of_csvs <- azkit::list_files(   azkit::get_container(     container_name = "supporting-data",     endpoint_url = Sys.getenv("AZ_STORAGE_EP")   ),   ext = "csv" )  list_of_csvs |>   # get the name from the file name but drop the extension detail   purrr::set_names(basename(tools::file_path_sans_ext(list_of_csvs))) |>   purrr::map(\(x) {     read_azure_csv(       azkit::get_container(         container_name = "supporting-data",         endpoint_url = Sys.getenv("AZ_STORAGE_EP")       ),       x     )   }) |>   list2env(.GlobalEnv)

title: Troubleshooting
text: Azure authentication probably main area might experience difficulty. debug, try running: look like: errors, ’s worth testing authentication set correctly using another package: successful authentication result following message: successfully used azkit get token run code may now multiple tokens can view: refresh token:
code: library(azkit)  token <- azkit::get_auth_token() Azure Active Directory v1.0 token for resource https://url.com   Tenant:   App ID:   Authentication method:   Token valid from: to:   MD5 hash of inputs: # install.packages("AzureRMR") library(AzureRMR)  # This will open the browser and prompt for a Microsoft Account to be used to connect AzureRMR::create_azure_login()  AzureRMR::get_azure_login() Loading Azure Resource Manager login for default tenant AzureRMR::list_azure_tokens() azkit::refresh_token(token)

title: Troubleshooting
text: get errors reading files, first check passing full correct file path relative root directory container. Next try reading raw data read_azure_file() returns binary form data (isn’t human readable). successful able pass data handler function ’s relevant data. Details can found Getting Started.

title: An alternative to stopifnot/assert_that etc — check_vec
code: check_vec(   x,   predicate,   message,   which = c("every", "some", "none"),   pf = parent.frame() )

title: An alternative to stopifnot/assert_that etc — check_vec
text: x object checked predicate predicate function used check elements x message custom error message, string. shown user predicate check succeed. Can include glued variables {cli} semantic markup. Variable values searched environment caller function (environment check_vec() ). makes easier include informative values message. One "every", "", "none", string. Defines {purrr} function use applying predicate. "every", default, means check_vec() succeed every value x satisfies predicate. "none" can used generate inverse predicate, situation success means none elements x satisfies predicate. "" unlikely useful often, available. pf Set parent.frame() variables caller environment can used custom error message.

what: Generate appropriate values for the resource parameter in get_auth_token — generate_resource
title: Generate appropriate values for the resource parameter in get_auth_token — generate_resource
text: helper function generate appropriate values. Ensure version argument matches aad_version argument get_auth_token. unlikely ever want set authorise FALSE option since AzureAuth::get_azure_token supports . Similarly, likely want keep refresh turned (argument effect v1 tokens, applies v2).

title: Generate appropriate values for the resource parameter in get_auth_token — generate_resource
code: generate_resource(   version = 1,   url = "https://storage.azure.com",   path = "/.default",   authorise = TRUE,   refresh = TRUE )

title: Generate appropriate values for the resource parameter in get_auth_token — generate_resource
text: version numeric. AAD version, either 1 2 (1 default) url URL Azure resource host path v2, path designating access scope authorise Boolean, whether return token authorisation scope, (TRUE, default) one just provides authentication. unlikely want turn refresh Boolean, applies v2 tokens , whether return token refresh token also supplied.

title: Generate appropriate values for the resource parameter in get_auth_token — generate_resource
text: scalar character, (v2 situations) character vector

title: Get Azure authentication token — get_auth_token
text: function retrieves Azure token specified resource. method avoids need refresh re-authenticating online. seems works v1 tokens. (v2 tokens always seem refresh via online re-authentication, refresh automatically.) instead generate completely fresh token, set force_refresh = TRUE get_auth_token

title: Get Azure authentication token — get_auth_token
code: get_auth_token(   resource = generate_resource(),   tenant = "common",   client_id = NULL,   auth_method = "authorization_code",   aad_version = 1,   force_refresh = FALSE,   ... )  refresh_token(token)

title: Get Azure authentication token — get_auth_token
text: resource v1, simple URL "https://storage.azure.com/" supplied. v2, vector specifying URL Azure resource token requested well desired scopes. See AzureAuth::get_azure_token details. Use generate_resource help provide appropriate string vector. setting version 2, ensure aad_version argument also set 2. set use AAD version 1 default. tenant string specifying Azure tenant. Defaults "common". See AzureAuth::get_azure_token values. client_id string specifying application ID (aka client ID). NULL, (default) function attempts obtain client ID Azure Resource Manager token, prompts user log obtain . auth_method string specifying authentication method. Defaults "authorization_code". use secret, pass "client_credentials" instead provide secret using password argument .... See AzureAuth::get_azure_token information. aad_version Numeric. AAD version, either 1 2 (1 default) force_refresh logical. Whether use stored token available (FALSE, default), try obtain new one Azure (TRUE). may useful wish generate new token resource value existing token, different tenant auth_method. Note can also try refresh_token, cause existing token refresh , without obtaining new token Azure via online reauthentication ... Optional arguments (eg token_args use_cache) passed AzureAuth::get_managed_token AzureAuth::get_azure_token, example overwrite opf default values supply password token Azure authentication token

title: Get Azure authentication token — get_auth_token
text: Azure token object Azure authentication token

title: Get Azure authentication token — get_auth_token
text: try get managed token used within managed resource Azure VM Azure App Service. method return token, try retrieve user token using provided parameters, requiring user authenticated using device. force_refresh set TRUE, fresh web authentication process launched. Otherwise attempt use cached token matching given resource, tenant aad_version.

title: Get Azure authentication token — get_auth_token
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get a token for the default resource token <- get_auth_token()  # Force generation of a new token via online reauthentication token <- get_auth_token(force_refresh = TRUE)  # Get a token for a specific resource and tenant token <- get_auth_token(   resource = "https://graph.microsoft.com",   tenant = "my-tenant-id" )  # Get a token using a specific app ID token <- get_auth_token(client_id = "my-app-id")  # Use a secret token <- get_auth_token(  tenant = "my-tenant-id",  client_id = "my-app-id",  auth_method = "client_credentials",  password = "123459878&%^" ) } # }

title: Read in data from an Azure table — read_azure_table
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Read all rows from a table read_azure_table(   "my_table",   table_endpoint = "https://myaccount.table.core.windows.net/" )  # Filter rows using an OData filter string read_azure_table(   "my_table",   table_endpoint = "https://myaccount.table.core.windows.net/",   filter = "PartitionKey eq 'my-partition'" )  # Filter, select specific properties, and limit the number of rows returned read_azure_table(   "my_table",   table_endpoint = "https://myaccount.table.core.windows.net/",   filter = "Status eq 'Active' and Score ge 10",   select = "PartitionKey,RowKey,Status,Score",   top = 100 ) } # }

title: azkit v0.2.0
text: ’s Changed - 🔥 Remove separate “managed_resource” argument get_auth_token() @francisbarton #41 - ❗Try surface error message Azure list_container_names() @francisbarton #40 - 🐛 Remove attempt support partial matching file names @francisbarton #36 - ☑️ Add check_container_class validation function @francisbarton #43 - Export check container function @francisbarton #54 - 🦡 Add hex badge (SVG versions) @francisbarton #55 - Add functions handle reading zipped json files “” file @francisbarton #57 - Add read_azure_table() function @francisbarton #58 - Add lintr appease @francisbarton #61 - Improve error messages user envvars found @francisbarton #63 - Rejig logic get_auth_token force_refresh turned @francisbarton #64 - Refactor function arguments handling default values env vars @francisbarton #68 - 🚢 Release v0.2 @francisbarton #69 - Full Changelog: v0.1.0…v0.2.0-
